comment: 65yo male active current smoker (30 pack-years cumulative, current ~1 ppd) with documented elevated residential radon exposure (home measurement 7.2 pCi/L, above EPA 4 pCi/L action threshold) in a geographic high-risk area (granite-bedrock region). No current lung-cancer diagnosis. Stacks two upstream universal/environmental RFs: (1) RF-UNIVERSAL-CURRENT-SMOKER-MULTI-CANCER via current_tobacco_use_documented → structured cessation (combined behavioural + varenicline / bupropion / NRT) + USPSTF LDCT eligibility (annual LDCT 50-80y / ≥20 pack-y / current or quit ≤15y — patient eligible); (2) RF-RADON-MALIGNANCY-PREVENTION via residential ≥4 pCi/L + documented high with smoking-hx branches → radon mitigation (sub-slab depressurization) + smoking-cessation amplification (radon-smoking synergy ~10-25x background lung-cancer risk per high cumulative exposure) + annual LDCT per modified USPSTF criteria. The radon-smoking interaction is greater-than-additive but submultiplicative on log-additive scale — cessation meaningfully attenuates radon component. Validates v0.3 multi-exposure stacking on the radon+tobacco synergy axis.
